topic: Cursor IDE memory files project context

sources.title: Cursor Rules Documentation
keyInsights: Four rule types: Project (.cursor/rules/), User, Team, AGENTS.md
MDC frontmatter fields: description, alwaysApply, globs
Rule application modes: always, intelligent, glob-scoped, manual
Keep rules under 500 lines; split into composable pieces
.cursorrules is deprecated; migrate to .cursor/rules/ or AGENTS.md
Precedence: Team Rules > Project Rules > User Rules

sources.title: Cursor Agent Overview
keyInsights: Agent uses Instructions + Tools + User messages
Fetch Rules tool allows dynamic rule retrieval
Automatic context summarization as conversations grow
/summarize command for manual context compression
No limit on tool calls during a task
Checkpoints created before modifications

sources.title: Cursor @Mentions Documentation
keyInsights: @Files, @Folders, @Code, @Docs, @Past Chats are main mention types
@Docs supports custom URLs and indexes all subpages
@Past Chats adds full prior conversation as context
Cursor 2.0 removed @Definitions, @Web, @Link, @Recent Changes, @Linter Errors
Agent gathers context automatically; manual attachment less needed

sources.title: Cursor Semantic Search Documentation
keyInsights: 7-step pipeline: sync, chunk, embed, store, query, compare, rank
Chunks are logical units: functions, classes, code blocks
Index refreshes every 5 minutes
Files matching .gitignore are excluded automatically
Large/complex files may be skipped for performance
Finds code by semantic meaning, not text matching

sources.title: Cursor Ignore Files Documentation
keyInsights: .cursorignore excludes from all AI features (semantic search, Tab, Agent, @mentions)
.cursorindexingignore excludes from index only; files remain accessible
Uses .gitignore syntax
Terminal and MCP tools can still access ignored files
Hierarchical search option for parent directory ignore files
Negation with ! prefix works with limitations

sources.title: Cursor Skills / SKILL.md Documentation
keyInsights: Skills are version-controlled packages with SKILL.md + optional scripts
Required frontmatter: name, description
Discovered from .cursor/skills/, ~/.cursor/skills/, marketplace
Agent applies contextually based on description
Invoked manually with /skill-name syntax
disable-model-invocation: true makes skill explicit-only

sources.title: Cursor Agent Hooks Documentation
keyInsights: Hooks are JSON-configured processes communicating via stdin/stdout
sessionStart hook can inject additional_context into system context
Command hooks (exit code 0=pass, 2=block) and prompt hooks
hooks.json hierarchy: enterprise > team > project > user
Separate hooks for Agent (chat) vs Tab (inline completion)

sources.title: Cursor MCP Documentation
keyInsights: MCP connects Cursor to external tools and data sources
Integrations: AWS docs, GitHub, Slack, Notion, Linear, databases
Extends context beyond token-based limits
Real-time access to live project data
Recommended for team-shared organizational knowledge

sources.title: Cursor Subagents Documentation
keyInsights: Subagents start with clean context; no conversation history inheritance
Parent agent must include all relevant context in the prompt
Foreground (blocking) and background (async) modes
Resume subagent by passing agent ID
Background subagents write state to files for parent to read

sources.title: Cursor Max Mode Documentation
keyInsights: Max Mode extends context to model's maximum supported window
Default window is ~200k tokens
20% surcharge on top of standard API rates
Most beneficial when model max exceeds 200k substantially
Useful for large codebase tasks requiring deep file traversal

sources.title: Cursor Agent Modes Documentation
keyInsights: Four modes: Agent (default), Ask (read-only), Plan, Debug
Agent mode: full tools, autonomous file editing and commands
Ask mode: search-only, no modifications
Plan mode: generates plans before writing code
Debug mode: hypothesis generation, log instrumentation, runtime analysis

sources.title: Cursor Core Concepts Documentation
keyInsights: Context = information provided to AI models: files, symbols, conversation history
Rules = custom instructions for coding standards and conventions
Chat supports multiple tabs, history, checkpoints, export
Semantic Search enables natural language code discovery
MCP connects AI to external data sources

sources.title: Cursor Quickstart Guide
keyInsights: Agent finds context automatically via search; no need to always tag files
Plans can be saved to .cursor/plans/ via 'Save to workspace'
Saved plans serve as documentation and future session context
@-mentions for explicit file attachment when location is known

sources.title: Cursor Agent Security Documentation
keyInsights: .cursorignore prevents sensitive file access by AI
Network restrictions: only GitHub, link retrieval, web search by default
Enterprise origin allowlists for agent browser access
Approval workflows before sensitive operations
MCP connections require user authorization

sources.title: Cursor Official Changelog
keyInsights: Skills and subagents added January 2026
Plugins/marketplace added February 2026
Long-running agents research preview February 2026
CLI agent modes (plan, ask, debug) added January 2026
No changes to rules/memory system in Jan-Feb 2026 window

sources.title: Cursor Agent Browser Documentation
keyInsights: Browser cookies, localStorage, IndexedDB persist across agent sessions within workspace
Visual screenshots integrated with file tools for UI context
Browser console, network logs accessible to agent
Session persistence provides implicit state across browser-enabled tasks

sources.title: awesome-cursorrules GitHub Repository
keyInsights: 38k+ stars; community standard template collection
Templates organized by framework: React, Vue, Angular, Next.js, Go, Python, etc.
Still uses .cursorrules format in examples (pre-deprecation templates)
Demonstrates common patterns: framework preferences, testing standards, API conventions
Content remains valid when migrated to .cursor/rules/ MDC format

sources.title: CursorRules.org Community Directory
keyInsights: AI-generated rule configs for React, Vue, Next.js and other frameworks
One-click deployment templates
Cross-file dependency mapping patterns in rules
Project-aware context retention patterns documented

sources.title: Builder.io: Cursor Tips
keyInsights: YOLO mode: allow automated test/build commands without approval gates
Test-driven approach: write tests first, then code, let agent iterate
Use Command K for quick inline edits without full context overhead
Add logging, share output - transforms abstract bugs into concrete data

sources.title: Cursor Rules - AGENTS.md section
keyInsights: AGENTS.md supports nested directories with hierarchical precedence
More specific (deeper) AGENTS.md takes precedence over parent
Plain markdown only - no frontmatter supported
Simpler than .cursor/rules/ but no conditional application
Cross-tool compatible with OpenCode, Codex, and other AI tools
